name: Chicken in Cream and Herb Sauce
recipeIngredient: 6 Chicken thighs (1 1/2 to 2 lbs), skinned and boned
All-purpose flour seasoned with salt and pepper to taste for dredging
2 tablespoons Minced fresh parsley, plus 2 tablespoons for garnish
recipeInstructions:
1. Pound chicken between sheets of plastic wrap with a heavy wooden mallet until about 1/4 inch thick.
2. Dredge the chicken in seasoned flour.
3. In a large skillet, heat 1 1/2 tablespoons butter and oil. Cook chicken pieces without crowding, cooking until the meat loses pinkness when slashed, about 1 1/2 minutes per side. Transfer to a hot platter and keep warm.
4. Cook remaining chicken pieces, adding more butter and oil as needed. Add to the platter and keep warm.
5. Add wine and lemon juice to the skillet and simmer over moderately high heat, stirring to blend in browned particles. Reduce to about half.
6. Stir in whipping cream, thyme, and parsley; boil until sauce thickens slightly.
7. Pour any meat juices from the hot platter into the sauce. Adjust seasoning as desired.
8. Pour the sauce over the chicken and garnish with parsley, lemon slices, and capers. Serves 6.
Note: Chicken breasts or veal may be substituted.
Source: Quick Italian Cuisine International, Knapp Press C 1984 ISBN 0-89535-147-1
